title: IATF webpage
summary: The public site for the International Accessibility Task Force.
problem: IATF needed a stable public home that explains the model, lists projects, and points contributors to the right next step.
why: The site is the public front door of IATF. It is built as a small static project so it stays easy to read, translate, and maintain in the open.
scopeIn: Public landing pages that explain the IATF model
Public registry of accepted, active, and archived IATF projects with status, maintainer, repository, and demo
A request intake page and form
English source with structure ready for translation
Static deployment on Cloudflare Workers Static Assets
scopeOut: Private member areas, accounts, or login flows
Hosting of project source code, which lives in each project's own repository
Real-time chat, which lives in Discord
Issue tracking for individual projects, which lives in each project's GitHub repository

title: IATF selfie
summary: An early IATF prototype, archived after reaching a natural stopping point.
problem: Early exploration of an accessibility-focused tool under the IATF umbrella.
why: The repository is kept public as a record of the work, even though active development has stopped.
scopeIn: Early prototype of an accessibility-focused tool kept as a public record

title: IATF bot
summary: An IATF community bot, archived after reaching a natural stopping point.
why: The repository is kept public as a record of the work, even though active development has stopped.
scopeIn: Discord automation around IATF community channels, kept as a public record
